title: देवी हृदयम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री देवी हृदयम् ॥

ॐ अस्य श्रीदेवीहृदयस्तोत्रस्य
भार्गव ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् छन्दः।
श्रीमहालक्ष्मीर्देवता।
ॐ ह्रीं बीजम्। श्रीं शक्तिः। ऐं कीलकम्।
श्रीदेवीप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
सर्वमङ्गलमाङ्गल्ये शिवे सर्वार्थसाधिके।
शरण्ये त्र्यम्बके गौरि नारायणि नमोऽस्तु ते॥

सिंहासनगतां नित्यं पद्माश्रितकरद्वयाम्।
शुभदां प्रणदां देवीं प्रणमामि सदा शिवाम्॥

॥ हृदयम् ॥
हृदयं देव्या ज्ञातव्यं सर्वसिद्धिप्रदायकम्।
गोपनीयं प्रयत्नेन सर्वैरपि सुरेश्वरि॥१॥

देवी सर्वभूतानां हृदये सम्प्रतिष्ठिता।
आकाशवत् सर्वगता विश्वव्यापिनि चेतना॥२॥

ॐ ह्रीं देव्यै हृदयाय नमः।
ॐ श्रीं देव्यै शिरसे स्वाहा।
ॐ ऐं देव्यै शिखायै वषट्।
ॐ क्लीं देव्यै कवचाय हुम्।
ॐ सौं देव्यै नेत्रत्रयाय वौषट्।
ॐ ह्स्रौं देव्यै अस्त्राय फट्॥३॥

दुर्गा दुर्गार्तिशमनी दुर्गापद्विनिवारिणी।
दुर्गमच्छेदिनी दुर्गसाधिनी दुर्गनाशिनी॥४॥

महादेवी महाकाली महालक्ष्मीर्महासुखा।
महासरस्वती पूज्या महाशक्तिर्महाप्रभा॥५॥

नवचण्डी महाचण्डी चामुण्डा चण्डविक्रमा।
चण्डदुर्गा विनिर्धूतशोकदुर्गा नमोऽस्तु ते॥६॥

ब्रह्मविष्णुशिवादीनां शक्तिरूपा सनातनी।
सृष्टिस्थितिलयकर्त्री देवीहृदयमुत्तमम्॥७॥

त्रिकालं यः पठेद्विद्वान् देवीहृदयमुत्तमम्।
सर्वसिद्धिमवाप्नोति देवीसायुज्यमाप्नुयात्॥८॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥
देवीहृदयमेतद्यः पठेद्भक्तिसमन्वितः।
सर्वापद्भ्यो विमुच्येत देवीलोकं स गच्छति।
सर्वसिद्धिं च कामांश्च लभते नात्र संशयः॥

॥ इति श्री देवी हृदयं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Devi Hridayam ||

Om Asya Shri Devi-hridaya-stotrasya
Bhargava Rishiph. Anushtup Chhandah.
Shri Mahalakshmeer-devata.
Om Hreem Bijam. Shreem Shaktih. Aim Kilakam.
Shri Devi-prityarthe Jape Viniyogah.

|| Dhyanam ||
Sarvamangala-mangalye Shive Sarvartha-sadhike.
Sharanye Tryambake Gauri Narayani Namostu Te.

Simhasana-gatam Nityam Padmashrita-karadvayam.
Shubhadam Pranadam Devim Pranamamhi Sada Shivam.

|| Hridayam ||
Hridayam Devya Jnatavyam Sarva-siddhi-pradayakam.
Gopaniyam Prayatnena Sarvair-api Sureshvari.||1||

Devi Sarvabhutanam Hridaye Sampratishthita.
Akashavat Sarvagata Vishvavyapini Chetana.||2||

Om Hreem Devyai Hridayaya Namah.
Om Shreem Devyai Shirase Svaha.
Om Aim Devyai Shikhayai Vashat.
Om Kleem Devyai Kavachaya Hum.
Om Saum Devyai Netratrayaya Vaushat.
Om Hsraum Devyai Astraya Phat.||3||

Durga Durgarti-shamani Durgapad-vinivaarini.
Durgamach-chhedini Durga-sadhini Durga-nashini.||4||

Mahadevi Mahakali Mahalakshmir-mahasukha.
Mahasaraswati Pujya Mahashaktir-mahaprabha.||5||

Navachandi Mahachandi Chamunda Chandavikrama.
Chanda-durga Vinirdhuta-shoka-durga Namostu Te.||6||

Brahma-Vishnu-Shivadinam Shakti-rupa Sanatani.
Srishti-sthiti-laya-kartri Devi-hridayam-uttamam.||7||

Trikalam Yah Pathedvidvan Devi-hridayam-uttamam.
Sarva-siddhim-avapnoti Devi-sayujyam-apnuyat.||8||

|| Phalashruti ||
Devi-hridayam-etadyah Pathed-bhakti-samanvitah.
Sarvapadbhyo Vimuchyeta Devilokam Sa Gachchhati.
Sarvasiddhim Cha Kamamscha Labhate Natra Samshayah.

|| Iti Shri Devi Hridayam Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning:
ध्यान: सर्वमंगलमांगल्ये, शिवे, सर्वार्थसाधिके, शरण्ये, त्र्यम्बके, गौरी, नारायणी को नमस्कार। सिंहासन पर विराजमान, पद्मधारिणी, शुभदा, प्रणदा, शिवा देवी को सदा प्रणाम।

१. देवी का हृदय सर्वसिद्धिप्रदायक है, इसे प्रयत्नपूर्वक गोपनीय रखना चाहिए।

२. देवी सर्वभूतों के हृदय में प्रतिष्ठित हैं, आकाश के समान सर्वव्यापी चेतना हैं।

३. ॐ ह्रीं से हृदय, श्रीं से शिर, ऐं से शिखा, क्लीं से कवच, सौं से नेत्र, ह्स्रौं से अस्त्र — देवी का अंगन्यास।

४. दुर्गा — दुर्गतिशमनी, दुर्गापदविनिवारिणी, दुर्गमच्छेदिनी, दुर्गसाधिनी, दुर्गनाशिनी।

५. महादेवी, महाकाली, महालक्ष्मी, महासुखा, महासरस्वती, महाशक्ति, महाप्रभा।

६. नवचण्डी, महाचण्डी, चामुण्डा, चण्डविक्रमा, चण्डदुर्गा — शोकनाशिनी दुर्गा को नमस्कार।

७. ब्रह्मा-विष्णु-शिव की शक्तिरूपा, सनातनी, सृष्टि-स्थिति-लय कर्त्री — यह उत्तम देवी हृदय है।

८. जो विद्वान त्रिकाल इस उत्तम देवी हृदय का पाठ करता है, वह सर्वसिद्धि प्राप्त करता है और देवीसायुज्य पाता है।

जो भक्तिपूर्वक इसका पाठ करता है, वह सर्वापदाओं से मुक्त होकर देवीलोक जाता है। सर्वसिद्धि और सब कामनाएँ प्राप्त होती हैं।

viniyog.shloka: ॐ अस्य श्रीदेवीहृदयस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य भार्गव ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्रीमहालक्ष्मी (देवी) देवता। ॐ ह्रीं बीजम्। श्रीं शक्तिः। ऐं कीलकम्। श्रीदेवीप्रीत्यर्थे देवीहृदयजपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: The heart essence (Hridayam) of the Divine Mother — supreme Shakti text
Contains Anganyasa with Shakti bija mantras (Hreem, Shreem, Aim, Kleem)
Covers all forms — Mahalakshmi, Mahakali, Mahasaraswati
Invokes sarva siddhi and union with the divine mother
Powerful during Navratri, Fridays, and Durga Puja
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Goddess Durga, Devi Hridayam (देवी हृदयम्) stands as a heart-essence prayer. Rooted in Atharvana tradition / Bhargava Rishi, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Regular recitation is believed to cover all forms — Mahalakshmi, Mahakali, Mahasaraswati, contain Anganyasa with Shakti bija mantras (Hreem, Shreem, Aim, Kleem), and grant Sarva Siddhi and union with the Divine Mother. This prayer is traditionally recited on Friday (Shukravar) and Tuesday (Mangalvar) and during Navratri and Durga Puja to invoke divine grace. Comprising 8 verses, it can be completed in approximately 7 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Devi Hridayam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Devi Hridayam in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. The heart essence of the Divine Mother with Shakti bija mantras for Sarva Siddhi.

seoFaqs.question: What is the source tradition of the Devi Hridayam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Devi Hridayam circulates in the broader Atharvana-Shakta literary tradition, alongside texts such as the Devi Atharvashirsha, Devi Suktam, Sri Suktam and the Bhavanopanishad. Several recensions exist; the most commonly cited version is associated with Sage Bhargava and is preserved in compendiums of Devi-stotras drawn from Pauranika and Tantric sources. As a heart-essence text, it distills the supreme identity of Devi as Adi-Shakti, the unmanifest source of Brahma, Vishnu, Shiva and the entire cosmos. The Hridayam is held in classical tradition though its full Pauranika provenance is less centrally fixed than longer texts like the Devi Mahatmyam. (Sources: Hindupedia Devi Stotra; Stotra Nidhi; Sanskritdocuments.org.)

seoFaqs.question: What does Hridayam mean in the title Devi Hridayam?
seoFaqs.answer: Hridayam literally means heart and in stotra terminology refers to the heart-essence prayer of a deity — the verses that are said to be most beloved to the deity and most directly express her inner nature. The Devi Hridayam therefore represents the prayer that approaches the Goddess at her most intimate level, beyond ritual offerings and elaborate liturgies, addressing her as Mata at the centre of all being. The Hridayam genre is found across deities (Lakshmi Hridaya, Vishnu Hridaya, Rama Hridaya, Hayagriva Hridaya), and the Devi Hridayam is the most prominent within the Shakta repertoire. (Sources: Hindupedia; Stotra Nidhi; Sanskritdocuments.)

seoFaqs.question: How is Devi Hridayam different from Chandi Hridayam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Chandi Hridayam is one of the six angas of the Durga Saptashati (Devi Mahatmyam) and is recited specifically as part of the Chandi Path liturgy from the Markandeya Purana. The Devi Hridayam is a more general heart-essence Devi stuti drawn from Atharvana-Shakta tradition and is not bound to the Saptashati framework — it can be recited as a standalone daily Devi prayer. Both invoke the same supreme Devi as Adi-Shakti, but the Chandi Hridayam emphasises her fierce Mahishasura-mardini aspect while the Devi Hridayam emphasises her cosmological identity as the unmanifest source of all. Many devotees recite both during Navaratri. (Sources: Hindupedia; Stotra Nidhi; Sanatan Sanstha.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the best time to recite Devi Hridayam?
seoFaqs.answer: Tuesday and Friday are the standard weekdays of Devi worship. Sharad Navaratri in Ashvin and Chaitra Navaratri in Chaitra are the supreme annual occasions, with the Hridayam recited on each of the nine nights ideally in conjunction with the Devi Mahatmyam, the Lalita Sahasranama or the Devi Atharvashirsha. Other recommended occasions include Ashtami of every fortnight, Purnima especially Sharad Purnima, the four annual Gupta Navaratras, and any morning after a clean bath as part of household Devi puja. Tantric practitioners may also recite it at sandhya and during sadhana sessions. (Sources: Drikpanchang Navratri; Hindupedia; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Devi Hridayam confer?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional commentaries on Devi heart-essence stotras attribute the following benefits to consistent recitation: removal of internal obstacles arising from kama, krodha and moha; protection from black magic, evil eye, planetary afflictions and untimely death; fulfilment of legitimate worldly desires through the combined grace of Mahalakshmi, Mahasaraswati and Mahakali; cultivation of the highest non-dual wisdom that recognises the Devi as one's own innermost Self; and ultimately moksha through dissolution of the individual ego into the Devi's universal Shakti. The Hridayam is particularly recommended for those seeking spiritual depth rather than purely material results. (Sources: Hindupedia Devi Atharvashirsha; Stotra Nidhi; Sanatan Sanstha.)

seoFaqs.question: Can householders and women recite Devi Hridayam without restriction?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes. As a Pauranika and Atharvana-tradition praise text, the Devi Hridayam is open to all devotees regardless of gender, age or caste. Householders typically recite it as part of morning puja, Friday Devi worship or Navaratri observances. Women in particular are considered direct embodiments of the Goddess and have always been encouraged in classical tradition to recite Devi stotras; the only customary suspension is mental rather than vocal recitation during the four days of monthly menstruation. Children may be taught the Hridayam from a young age as a foundational Devi prayer. (Sources: Hindupedia; Sanatan Sanstha; Stotra Nidhi.)
